title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Donald Craig, a doctor, confides in Dr. McMaster regarding his concerns about his girlfriend, Hester Aigle. Donald theorizes that Hester may have killed her mother, Mrs. Aigle, with a poker after overhearing an argument between her mother and Jacqueline. He suggests that Hester's motive was not cold-blooded murder, but rather an impulsive act of emotional rebellion intended to escape her mother’s suffocating and dominant personality. Donald recounts Hester's history of insecurity and her failed attempt to assert independence through a theater troupe, which ended when she became involved with a middle-aged man.

McMaster acknowledges that seeking freedom from a dominant figure is a possible motive, noting that Mrs. Aigle's death effectively liberated several people, including Leo, Mary, and Mickey, from her personal and financial control. Despite the psychological complexities, Donald insists he must know the truth before marrying Hester, claiming he would still love her even if she were guilty. McMaster warns that such a revelation could destroy the trust in their relationship.

In a separate discussion, Leo Aigle, Mary Darrant, and Marshall discuss a recent newspaper advertisement that is expected to reignite press interest in the murder case. Marshall advises a policy of "no comment" toward reporters. Leo mentions that Detective Chief Inspector Rusey intends to visit to discuss the matter. They reflect on the difficulty of reopening an investigation two years later, as local villagers may have forgotten key details. Mary Darrant notes that while the door was locked against thieves, someone could have been let in by her mother, observing that her father heard the doorbell ring at seven o'clock.
